_comment: ABOUTME: Evidence-pool template — one JSON object per line. The orchestrator copies this file to docs/research-report/<topic>/evidence-pool.jsonl on first iteration and appends entries thereafter. Researcher agents return entries that the orchestrator validates and appends. The narrative-writer reads this file (filtered by chapter) when drafting body chapters.

FORMAT: One JSON object per line (.jsonl). Each entry must include: id, claim, evidence_summary, narrowest_defensible_reading, source_assertion, source_keys, gap_rating, load_bearing_assumptions, regime_conditions, themes, supports_chapter_arg, confidence, iteration_added, strategy. Strategy-specific fields (verification, resolution, counter_argument, gap_assessment, temporal_context, cross_domain) are added per the researcher agent's output spec.
EXAMPLE ENTRY (delete on real use — this file is a template, not actual data):

claim: Roughly 60% of US dental practices report using some form of revenue-cycle automation as of 2024.
evidence_summary: ADA's 2024 practice-management survey of 1,200 practices found 58% reported using at least one automated RCM tool. Becker's 2024 industry report cited a similar 55-62% range from a separate vendor-side survey.
narrowest_defensible_reading: Two separate surveys conducted in 2024 found prevalence of any RCM automation in the 55-62% range among surveyed US dental practices.
source_assertion: ADA's report headline framed this as 'majority adoption of automation', which is broader than what the prevalence numbers alone show — adoption depth and integration quality vary widely.
load_bearing_assumptions: Survey respondents accurately self-report tool usage
The surveyed practices are representative of US dental practices broadly (both surveys oversample larger DSO-affiliated practices)
regime_conditions: US-only, 2024 data, surveyed practices skew toward larger and DSO-affiliated; small independent practices likely underrepresented.
